message:
perf(sync): optimize waiter removal with pop_front fast-path in resource pool

When removing a waiter after acquiring a resource or creating a new one,
the pool previously always used VecDeque::retain() which scans the entire
queue. Since waiters are typically served in FIFO order, the waiter being
removed is almost always at the front.

Add a fast-path: check if the front element matches first and use
pop_front() (O(1)) before falling back to retain() (O(n)). This avoids
unnecessary linear scans in the common case.

Applied in both acquire_idle_or_none() and try_create_new() paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
